0_TextBox_3:
Assistant Professor of Radiology

Brigham and Women’s Hospital

Harvard Medical School
1_Goal_body: Este tutorial es una breve introducción al módulo de bienvenida del software de código abierto Slicer.
2_TextBox_1:
*Slicer is an open-source software for segmentation, registration and visualization of medical imaging data.
*The platform is developed through a multi-institution effort of several NIH funded large-scale consortia.
*Slicer is for medical research only, and is not FDA approved.
3_TextBox_1: 3D Slicer 5 version 5.10.0 includes over 100 modules and more than 190 extensions for image segmentation, registration and 3D visualization of medical imaging data.
4_TextBox_1:
Panel de interfaz
de usuario (IU) del
módulo de bienvenida
de Slicer
5_TextBox_1:
Each module of Slicer includes a series of tabs, which give access to different functionalities.

Click on the arrow symbol to display the content of each tab.
6_TextBox_3: User Interface (UI) panel of the Slicer Welcome Module
8_TextBox_0:
The Welcome module panel contains shortcuts for loading different types of data. A series of sample data are also available.

Click on Download Sample Data to access the Sample Data Module
9_TextBox_1: The Sample Data module contains links to different sample datasets that can be downloaded into Slicer.
11_ArrowText_0:
Click on MRHead to download the
dataset in Slicer.
12_TextBox_0:
The MR scan of the brain appears
in the 2D viewers.
